feed.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions

entry.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
entry.summary:
The discussion around Bitcoin development resources highlights several valuable points for anyone interested in the field. StackExchange has been identified as a crucial platform where developers can engage and share knowledge about Bitcoin. Additionally, the website "Learn me a Bitcoin" is recommended as an essential resource for understanding the intricacies of Bitcoin technology. The suggestion to utilize these resources by extracting information into smaller, manageable papers could be beneficial for those seeking specific guidance or advice. This approach might also involve using Retrievable Answer Generation (RAG) resources to enhance the accessibility and utility of the information.
Moreover, there is an emphasis on the importance of proper attribution. However, it remains unclear whether this refers to the attribution of sources, actions by Language Learning Models (LLMs), or another aspect entirely. Clarification on this point is necessary to ensure that all uses of gathered information adhere to ethical standards and proper source acknowledgment.
